pricing.with_insurance: As low as $25 + $90 physician oversight fee
pricing.price_positioning: Premium but insurance-eligible. Lead with insurance price in ads
pricing.note: Price from infinitewellnessusa.com DTC store. LabFinder lists $648 (different channel)

packaging.material: Matte white cardboard, rigid (not flexible like LYFEfuel pouch)

dimensions.notes: Approximate based on standard medical test kit sizing

label_elements: INFINITE wordmark at top center (navy, spaced letters, stylized M)
Large geometric logo center (infinity/M shaped abstract mark, navy)
FOOD ALLERGY & SENSITIVITY TEST at bottom center (navy, all caps, smaller than logo)
packaging.visual_description: Extremely clean and minimal. White box with ONLY navy branding. No photos, no busy graphics, no color gradients. Just logo + product name. Reads as medical/professional, not consumer/flashy
packaging.scale_in_hand: Box width is roughly 1.5x a hand width. When held in one hand, fingers wrap around the short edge. When held in two hands like opening a gift, it covers both palms. Slightly larger than a hardcover book
packaging.weight_feel: Light rigid box. Does not sag or flex. Feels like a premium packaging box

reference_images.notes: White rectangular box, landscape orientation. Navy INFINITE wordmark at top, large navy geometric logo (infinity/M shape) center, 'FOOD ALLERGY & SENSITIVITY TEST' in navy at bottom. Clean, minimal, medical-professional. Studio shot on grey background with soft shadow

immune_pathways.IgE: Immediate allergic reactions (minutes to hours). Histamine, hives, breathing issues, anaphylaxis
immune_pathways.IgG: Delayed food sensitivities (3-72 hours). Hard to identify without testing
immune_pathways.C3b_d: Complement system. Amplifies IgG reactions 1,000 to 10,000x. Tests that only measure IgG miss these

what_it_tests.key_claim: Tests 4 immune pathways. Most competitors only test 1-2. This catches reactions other tests miss

collection_methods.option_1: Dried Blood Spot (DBS) — simple at-home finger prick. Easy, safe, mail back
collection_methods.option_2: Serum collection — requires phlebotomist (blood draw at lab/clinic)

results.format: Color-coded report, food groupings, single-page overview, detailed allergen list, foods-to-avoid list
results.followup: Free consultation with registered dietitian included

strongest_hooks: The 'finally got answers' relief angle (years of mystery symptoms)
The hidden food trigger reveal (foods you eat daily causing issues)
The '4 pathways' superiority (most tests miss delayed reactions)
The simplicity angle (finger prick at home, results in days)
The insurance angle ($25 with insurance vs $648 self-pay)
The symptom connection (your bloating/fatigue/skin ISN'T normal)
proof_points: CLIA certified, COLA accredited (real lab credentials)
emotional_triggers: Frustration of not knowing what's causing symptoms
Empowerment of taking control of your health
Surprise at discovering hidden food triggers
Validation that symptoms were real (not 'in your head')
